footer.tagline: Slack-native access management. Ask, approve, audit — without leaving the chat.

hero.statusPillDesktop: Slack-first access management · First month free
hero.tagline: Manage your team's SaaS access in Slack — starting with /cookie like {'@'}teammate.
hero.finePrint: 30 days free · No card required · Slack admin install only

employee.line: Give your team the access they need, the moment they need it.

admin.line: Stop being the bottleneck for every access request.

toolsStrip.eyebrow: Works with any SaaS tool out of the box. Request any with /cookie.
toolsStrip.framing: Cookie Jar manages access to SaaS apps directly in Slack. The audit trail is in the dashboard.

whySlack.title: Because that's where the work already is.
bullets: Lives where the request actually happens — no new tool to learn.
Five-minute install, no IdP required.
Approvals in the same DM thread the requester is already in.
The dashboard holds the audit trail and access overview. Slack is for the loop, the dashboard is for the record.

whoForHome.title: Built for the messy middle.
whoForHome.body: Cookie Jar is for teams of roughly 10–100 people, on Slack, where the founder or an engineering lead is still the one approving access requests. If that's you, you'll feel at home.
whoForHome.notForBody: companies on Microsoft Teams, or enterprises with a dedicated IAM team running Okta or Sailpoint — those tools already solve the problem better at that scale.

standout1.titleLine1: "What tools does the new hire need?"
standout1.bodyAfter: to see Sarah's tools as checkboxes. Tick the ones you want, hit Request. Owners get pinged for each tool — one click each to approve.
bullets: See your teammate's shelf as a checklist
Batch the request, owners review one DM each
Auto-approved tools skip the queue entirely

standout2.body: Assign one or more owners to each tool from the dashboard. When someone asks for Figma, the head of design gets the DM — not the CTO. Multi-owner committees supported; first click resolves the request, the others' DMs auto-update.
bullets: Per-tool ownership, not per-team
Multi-owner fan-out — any one resolves it
Admins are a fallback, not the default

standout3.bodyBefore: Most access tools are web apps that send Slack pings. Cookie Jar is a Slack app that has a web dashboard. Type
standout3.bodyAfter: and the full command surface is right there — request, list, configure, audit. The dashboard is for monthly review, not daily ops.
bullets: Zero new UI for employees to learn
Approve in Slack. Audit on the dashboard.
CTO opens the dashboard maybe once a month

proRoadmap.ledeBefore: All of these ship as part of Pro. Pro customers get them as they land — no add-on pricing, no per-feature upsell.
proRoadmap.ledeStrong: Per-tool cost tracking, quarterly access reviews, and smart audit search are built on request for Family Size.

items.title: /cookie requests
items.body: Read-heavy command for owners reviewing the queue. Comes back to Slack on Pro alongside warm functions.

items.title: Auto-approve rules
items.body: Pre-approve trusted patterns — per company, per team, per tool. Most-restrictive-wins precedence; safe-deny on errors.

items.title: Sub-second slash commands
items.body: Pro keeps Cookie Jar warm so /cookie never times out. No more 1–3s cold-start window.

items.title: Audit browser
items.body: Paginated, filterable table in the dashboard. Action-type colour coding, day grouping, CSV export.

items.title: Dashboard bulk revoke
items.body: Right now revoke happens in Slack. Bringing it to the dashboard with bulk select + filter.

items.title: Per-member overrides
items.body: Default by team, exceptions by person. "Bob in Engineering, but no GitHub admin."

items.title: Auto-provisioning
items.body: Skip the manual step. Cookie Jar adds the user in GitHub, Jira, Figma for you via n8n integrations.

items.title: Owner suggestions
items.body: "{'@'}sarah-tech-lead has GitHub. Make her the owner?" Suggested during install based on existing access patterns.

items.title: Encryption
items.body: TLS in transit, AES-256 at rest. Slack bot tokens are encrypted with rotation support.

items.title: Signed webhooks
items.body: Every request from Slack is verified with HMAC-SHA256 and a 5-minute replay window before we trust it.

items.title: Workspace isolation
items.body: Your data is enforced-isolated at the database layer. Admins only see their own org.

items.title: Role-based access
items.body: Every admin operation checks the operator's role on the workspace, every time.

items.title: Rate limiting
items.body: Public endpoints are rate-limited to prevent abuse.

items.title: Audit log on every action
items.body: Grant, revoke, approve, deny, admin promotion. Recorded with actor, target, tool, and timestamp.

items.title: OAuth installer gate
items.body: Only Slack workspace admins or owners can install Cookie Jar — preventing employees from auto-promoting themselves.

trust.honestyBody: Cookie Jar is not yet SOC 2 certified. We follow the practices that lead there and will pursue formal certification as customers require it. We don't make claims we can't back up.

items.a: Not yet. We follow the controls that lead to SOC 2 — encryption, signed webhooks, workspace isolation, RBAC, audit logging — and will pursue formal certification when a customer's procurement requires it. We won't claim a certification we don't hold.
United States, on Google Cloud infrastructure. If you're in the EEA or UK, we rely on standard contractual clauses for cross-border transfer.
Yes. Email us and we'll send our standard DPA. For larger customers, we can negotiate.

items.q: Why do you need email permission from Slack?
items.a: The users:read.email scope is used to map Slack users to Google SSO sign-ins on the dashboard. We don't use it for anything else.

items.q: What happens if Slack tokens leak?
items.a: Tokens are encrypted at rest with rotation support. If a leak is suspected, admins can rotate by reinstalling the app — that revokes the old token immediately. We notify affected workspaces within 24 hours of confirming an incident involving your data.

items.q: Can a regular employee install Cookie Jar to give themselves admin?
items.a: No. Cookie Jar checks at OAuth time — only Slack workspace admins or owners can install. Employee installs are rejected with a "ask your workspace admin" message.

items.q: Does Cookie Jar actually grant access in GitHub, Jira, Figma — or does someone still need to do it manually?
items.a1: Today, Cookie Jar runs the workflow: request, route, approve, audit. The owner who clicks "Approve" still goes to GitHub (or wherever) and adds the user themselves. It's a 30-second step they're already in the headspace for, since they just made the decision.
items.a2: Auto-provisioning — actually creating the seat in the third-party tool when an approval lands — is in the Pro roadmap. Until that ships, Cookie Jar is the source of truth for who has been approved for what, and the manual provisioning step is honest and visible in the Slack flow.

items.q: Who can install Cookie Jar in our Slack workspace?
items.a1: Only Slack workspace admins or owners. Cookie Jar checks at OAuth time — if the installer isn't a Slack admin, the install is rejected with a clear "ask your workspace admin to install this for you" message.
items.a2: Whoever installs becomes the first Cookie Jar admin. They can promote other employees to admin from the dashboard (head of engineering, head of design, etc.). The installer doesn't have to manage everything alone.

items.a1: About five minutes from "Add to Slack" to first access request flowing through Cookie Jar.
items.a2: The flow: install → Cookie Jar pre-loads 24 common tools (GitHub, Jira, Figma, Notion, AWS, and more) → assign owners to the tools that have specific managers → done. Auto-approve rules, team channel mapping, and everything else can come later, configured from Slack with no dashboard required.
items.a3: There's no HRIS to connect, no IdP to wire up, no agent to install on anyone's machine.

items.q: What if a tool we use isn't in your catalog?
items.a1: Type /cookie shelf add notion (or whatever the tool is) and it's on the shelf. Cookie Jar doesn't care what the tool actually is — the catalog is just a list of names you decide on. Internal tools, niche SaaS, custom apps, all welcome. The owner-routing and approval flow work identically regardless of whether the tool is "popular" or one you invented internally.

items.q: Do you integrate with our HRIS or IdP?
items.a1: No, and that's deliberate. Cookie Jar is built for 10–50-person startups that don't have a Workday, BambooHR, Okta, or AzureAD setup yet — exactly the segment that's too small for AccessOwl ($250/mo minimum) or Cakewalk (requires HRIS).
items.a2: Slack is the source of truth for "who works here." Cookie Jar runs on top of that. Once your team grows past the point where this makes sense, you'll graduate to a heavier tool — that's the right outcome.

items.q: What happens to our data if we uninstall Cookie Jar?
items.a1: Uninstalling immediately removes our access credentials to your Slack workspace — we can't post or read anything in your Slack as soon as you click "Remove." Your historical access records, audit log, and member profiles stay in Cookie Jar's database until you request deletion.
items.a2: To delete everything, email us. Full details in our Privacy Policy.

finalCta.lede: First month free. Five minutes to install.

setup.lede: No IT ticket. No procurement call. One Slack admin clicks Add to Slack — three steps later, your team is requesting cookies.

steps.title: Click Add to Slack
steps.body: Workspace admin or owner installs Cookie Jar in one click. 24 common tools are pre-loaded — GitHub, Figma, Jira, Notion, AWS, and 19 more.

steps.title: Assign owners to tools
steps.body: Open the Shelf in the dashboard. Add one or more owners per tool — head of design owns Figma, head of eng owns GitHub. Two minutes saves a hundred DMs.

steps.title: Your team grabs cookies
steps.body: Anyone types /cookie [tool] in Slack. Owners get a DM with Approve / Deny buttons. One click resolves the request. Audit log captures it.

setup.finePrint: 30 days free · No card required · Slack admin install only

dashboard.lede: Person cards. Tool badges. Filter, search, audit. The dashboard is for monthly review — not daily ops. Your team lives in Slack; you check in here when you want the full picture.
bullets: One row per person, badges for every tool they hold
Click any tool to see grant history, owner, and audit trail
Export to CSV for compliance reviews

realSlackOutput.lede: No stylized renders. These are the ephemeral messages Cookie Jar posts back when you run a command — same copy, same structure.

demos.desc: When a tool isn't on the shelf, Cookie Jar suggests the closest match and lists the full shelf.
Your jar. Every tool you have, who granted it, in one ephemeral list.
Anyone in your workspace can peek at a teammate's jar — basis for /cookie like.
Admin view of every cookie on the shelf, its approval policy, and the owner if there is one.

items.title: One-click approve / deny
items.body: Owners get a Slack DM with [Approve] [Deny] buttons. One click resolves the request, the audit log captures the decision, and the requester gets DM'd the outcome.

items.title: /cookie like {'@'}teammate
items.body: Clone a teammate's tool list in one command. Type /cookie like {'@'}sarah, see her tools as checkboxes, tick what the new hire needs, hit Request. Owners get pinged for each tool — one click each to approve.

items.title: 24 tools pre-loaded
items.body: Jira, GitHub, Figma, Notion, Linear, AWS, GCP, Datadog, Sentry, Stripe, and 14 more. Plus your own custom tools — up to 3 on Starter, up to 20 on Pro, unlimited on Family Size.

items.title: Real-time access overview
items.body: Dashboard view of who has what across the org. Person cards with tool badges. Filter, search, see at a glance.

items.title: Audit log on every action
items.body: Every grant, revoke, approve, deny, admin promotion — recorded with actor, target, tool, and timestamp. Browseable view in Slack and dashboard coming soon.

items.title: Onboarding DM for new joiners
items.body: When someone joins your Slack workspace, Cookie Jar auto-DMs them with a starter command list. They self-serve from minute one.

offboarding.titleLine2: empty the jar in one command.
offboarding.bodyAfter: and Cookie Jar will list every tool Bob has, who owns each one, and what it costs you per month — and DM each owner a revoke checklist. No Notion page, no spreadsheet, no Friday-evening guesswork.
bullets: Complete picture of someone's access in one place
Owners get a per-tool revoke checklist in Slack
Per-tool cost surfaced so you know what you save
offboarding.honestyBefore: Not yet built. Today, offboarding is manual — you check the dashboard for who has what and ask each owner to revoke (same shape as adding a seat). The
offboarding.honestyAfter: auto-flow ships on Pro alongside auto-provisioning.

steps.title: An employee asks for access in Slack
steps.body: They type /cookie request github (or /cookie like {'@'}sarah for a batch). Cookie Jar parses the command and confirms the tool exists on the shelf.

steps.title: Cookie Jar pings the right owner
steps.body: It looks up the owner(s) for that tool and DMs them with [Approve] [Deny] buttons. Falls back to admins if no owner is assigned.

steps.title: The owner clicks Approve in their DM
steps.body: One click resolves the request. Multi-owner committees fan out — whoever clicks first wins, the others' DMs auto-update so nobody double-approves.

steps.title: The owner adds the seat in the actual tool
steps.body: A 30-second manual step in GitHub, Figma, or wherever. The owner is already in the headspace — they just decided yes.

steps.title: Cookie Jar logs it and notifies the requester
steps.body: The audit log captures actor, target, tool, and timestamp. The requester gets the outcome in Slack. The dashboard updates in real time.

pipeline.roadmapCalloutBody: Auto-provisioning (step 04) ships as part of Pro, via n8n integrations to GitHub, Jira, and Figma first.
pipeline.offboardingTitle: Offboarding works the same way, in reverse.
pipeline.offboardingBody: When someone leaves, the admin opens the dashboard to see what they have, then pings each owner to revoke. Same shape as granting a seat: Cookie Jar gives you the picture, the click is still in the third-party tool. Auto-revoke is on the Pro roadmap.

problem.headline: Managing SaaS access without an IT team.
problem.lede: Every access request is a tax on two people. Here's where it hurts.

employee.headline: Finding who owns GitHub, Figma, or AWS takes too long.
bullets: You don't know who's admin — CTO? Eng lead? Someone who left?
You DM the founder and hope it's the right person.
Then you wait. And bump. And wait.

owner.sceneTitle: Won't have a chance to read their message today.
owner.headline: Approving access requests breaks your deep work.
bullets: Slack pings interrupt every block of real work.
Good chance your message isn't read for hours or more.
Even if they do read, they may need to DM someone else to give access.

whoFor.title: Built for the teams between scrappy and SOC 2.

items.title: Startups without IT
items.body: You're 10–50 people, no dedicated admin, and access requests land in someone's DMs.

items.title: Engineering-led teams
items.body: Your founders or eng leads approve tool access today. Cookie Jar gives them a one-click path instead of a Notion doc.

items.title: SaaS sprawl, 10–100 people
items.body: You've got 30+ tools across the workspace and nobody can answer who has Figma.

items.title: Prepping for SOC 2
items.body: Auditors want an access log. Cookie Jar gives you a queryable record per tool, per person, per date.

pricing.lede: Two flat-rate paid tiers with a one-month free trial on either. $19/mo for the basics, $49/mo for the works, or Family Size for teams of 30+. No perpetual free tier.

toggle.monthlyHint: Switch to yearly anytime to save 2 months.

starter.name: Starter
starter.tagline: The basics. The floor. Everything you need to stop running access through DMs.
starter.monthlyMeta: First month free, no card required. Subscribe from the dashboard when you're ready.
starter.yearlyMeta: Billed annually at $190 (pay 10 months, get 12). First month free, no card required.
starter.metaSub: Flat per company up to 30 active members. Cancel anytime from the dashboard.
featuresIncluded: Audit log captured on every action (dashboard view coming soon)
24 pre-loaded tools + up to 3 of your own custom tools
featuresExcluded: Pro roadmap features as they ship (see Pro)

pro.name: Pro
pro.tagline: The works. More room in the jar — bigger catalog, priority support, every new feature as it ships.
pro.monthlyMeta: First month free, no card required. Subscribe from the dashboard when you're ready.
pro.yearlyMeta: Billed annually at $490 (pay 10 months, get 12). First month free, no card required.
pro.metaSub: Flat per company up to 30 active members. Cancel anytime from the dashboard.
featuresIncluded: Custom tools — up to 20 of your own beyond the 24 pre-loaded; swap freely as your stack changes
Continuous updates — every new Pro feature included as it ships
Direct line to the product team to shape what we build next
pro.pipelineListTitle: In the pipeline for Pro (coming soon)
featuresPipeline: /cookie requests — read-heavy command for owners reviewing the queue

familySize.name: Family Size
familySize.tagline: Big batch. For teams whose size, stack, or compliance needs outgrow the standard tiers.
familySize.meta: Custom pricing — flat per company, scaled to your size and needs. Quick call, no slides.
featuresIncluded: Teams over 30 active members — no member cap
Custom pricing — flat per company, scaled to size + needs
Custom roadmap — Family Size feature requests jump to the front of the build queue
familySize.onRequestListTitle: Built on request for Family Size
featuresOnRequest: Quarterly access reviews with smart revoke suggestions

pricing.flatNote: Flat per company, not per seat. Starter and Pro handle teams up to 30 active members. Past 30, you're on Family Size.

howItWorks.title: From install to billing, in plain English.

steps.head: Install Cookie Jar in your Slack workspace
steps.body: Installer becomes the first admin. We pull your email from Slack — no separate signup form.

steps.head: Try it free for 30 days
steps.body: Full Pro features unlocked during the trial. No card required to install — set up your shelf, assign owners, run /cookie.

steps.head: Subscribe before day 31 to keep going
steps.body: Pick Starter or Pro from the dashboard. We email you a Stripe payment link. Pay it and your plan is on.

steps.head: Cancel anytime from the dashboard
steps.body: Your access continues until the end of the billing period. No partial-month refunds.

howItWorks.footnote: We process payments through Stripe. We don't store card information.

items.q: Is there a free tier forever?
items.a: No. Cookie Jar is a paid product after the first free month. Starter at $19 is intentionally low so smaller teams aren't priced out — but everyone who keeps using Cookie Jar pays something. This keeps us focused on actual customers, not free riders.

items.q: What if I want to cancel during the trial?
items.a: Cancel any time from the dashboard. Since no card is required to install, there's nothing to charge — just stop using Cookie Jar or uninstall from Slack.
Yes, anytime from the dashboard. Upgrades take effect immediately and we prorate the difference. Downgrades take effect at the end of the current billing period.

items.q: Does pricing scale with my team size?
items.a: Starter and Pro are flat per company up to 30 active members — a 5-person team pays the same as a 30-person team. Past 30, you're on Family Size, which is custom-priced based on team size and what you need (SSO, residency, compliance evidence, etc.).

items.q: How do you count team size?
items.a1: Active human members of your Slack workspace — what Slack's users.list returns, minus bots, Slack apps, and deactivated accounts.
items.a2: If your team grows past 30, reach out and we'll switch you to Family Size pricing.

items.a: Cancel anytime; you keep access through the end of the period you've paid for. We don't issue partial-month refunds, but if something's broken on our end, contact us and we'll make it right.

items.q: Why is Pro more than Starter?
items.a: Pro is the upgrade lane: custom tools beyond the 24 pre-loaded (up to 20 of your own), priority support, and every new Pro feature included as it ships — /cookie requests, auto-approve rules, the audit browser, and warm functions are all on the Pro roadmap. Starter is the floor: everything you need to run access through Slack instead of DMs. If you only use the pre-loaded tool catalog and don't need the upcoming power features, Starter is enough.

biggerTeam.title: Bigger team? Bigger jar.
biggerTeam.body: 30+ people, regulated industry, or need things like SSO/SAML, on-premise deployment, custom data residency, or formal compliance evidence? Schedule a Family Size call — we'll work it out together.
